emergent_themes.description: Deliberate resistance to letting convenience become habit to maintain treat status and financial control
Fear that subscriptions create psychological pressure to order more frequently to justify the cost
Frustration with inaccurate dietary filtering leading to wasted time and disappointment
How gaining disposable income from first job dramatically changes ordering frequency and platform relationship

coding_summary: Alexandra represents a recent graduate in transition, showing how major life changes (graduation, first job, becoming vegan) reshape delivery habits. Her relationship with delivery is deliberately controlled - she actively resists letting it become habitual to preserve its 'treat' status. Price sensitivity drives all decisions, with systematic comparison across platforms. Strong subscription resistance stems from psychological concerns about pressure to order more. Dietary restrictions create both restaurant loyalty and app frustration. Her post-graduation income increase has enabled more frequent ordering, but within careful boundaries.
